answer:
The correct answer is G. Under Article I, Section 8, Clause 17 of the Constitution, Congress has plenary legislative authority over the District of Columbia, which includes the power to regulate its streets. This federal statute therefore supersedes any conflicting local ordinance passed by the D.C. City Council. The statute is valid regardless of Congress’s motive, as its authority over the District is exclusive and complete.  

\boxed{G}
